overallInterpretation: The screenshot shows a terminal/console text output titled 'GPU Physics Demo: Bouncing Spheres'. It displays WebGPU device initialization, compute shader compilation, creation of 500 rigid bodies, dispatching of 4 shader passes (clear grid, populate, collision, integration), position readback results for several bodies, a verification section, and a GPU performance measurement line (~60 frames in ~958ms). The output ends with 'Demo complete'. However, this is entirely text-based console output -- there is no visual browser rendering of spheres bouncing in a box. The expected demo specifies a 'Browser demo: 500+ bouncing spheres in a box' which implies a visual scene with rendered geometry, not a text log of GPU compute results.

artifacts.interpretation: Terminal/console text output showing GPU physics pipeline execution: WebGPU init, 4 compute shader passes dispatched, body position readbacks, and a performance summary. No visual rendering of spheres is present -- only text-based logging of the compute pipeline results.
